summary: Pure decision organ: Gmail OAuth credential freshness / connection gate. Given a pre-loaded token bundle + clock + requested scopes, decides the single next auth action (not_connected | refresh | use_cached) without touching Google's client libraries, the network, or a DB. Extracted from discovery-engine lib/dataflow_core/gmail_inbox/auth.py.

_checked.note: Corpus: overlaps organ-gmail-credentials-credentials — both decide Gmail OAuth token-refresh routing from {refresh_token present, expires_at <= now + 60s skew} → not_connected/refresh/use. The credentials organ is the superset (adds OAuth client-config completeness). They mirror two source-level near-duplicate modules (gmail_inbox/auth.py vs gmail_credentials/credentials.py). See flagged issue. OSS: NO_ADOPT — the pure 60s-skew freshness decision is trivial bespoke glue mirroring the host's exact rule; the real OAuth refresh I/O already delegates to google-auth in the caller, and the stdlib-only organ-purity contract bars adding the dep here.
